content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
/ 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Ethan Bertrand 
Isla Vista Community Services District 
clo Santa Barbara Co. 
Third District Supervisor's Office 
105 East Anaparnu Street 
Santa Barbara, CA 93101 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-17-125 
Dear Mr. Bertrand: 
August 24, 2017 
This letter is written notification that we have withdrawn the above-captioned request for 
advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act.1 
As explained in our three prior requests via electronic and U.S. mail, we required additional 
information to analyze your request for advice. As of the date of this letter, we have not received a 
response from you. Therefore, your request has been withdrawn. You may resubmit your request 
for advice if in the future, you can provide the additional information we requested. 
Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
ECR:jgl 
Sincerely, 
c:::: /1/ 
11/ 
L:;_;--C::::--  p. /4
A
.,_, 
// 
By: 
Emelyn C. Rodriguez 
· 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997 
of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor had submitted a question regarding the Political Reform Act, but the specific legal question is not detailed in this withdrawal letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice due to the requestor's failure to provide requested additional information after three prior requests via electronic and U.S. mail.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no Question or Conclusion sections. The letter clearly indicates this is a withdrawal due to lack of response to information requests. The original question cannot be determined from this document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor had submitted a question regarding the Political Reform Act, but the specific legal question is not detailed in this withdrawal letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice due to the requestor's failure to provide requested additional information after three prior requests via electronic and U.S. mail.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew an advice request (File No. W-17-125) from Ethan Bertrand of Isla Vista Community Services District because the requestor failed to provide additional information required for analysis after three prior requests.
